section.heading: USGS energy and minerals research facility

content.p: The Director of the United States Geological Survey (referred to in this section as the “Director”),11 So in original. The comma probably should not appear. shall fund, through a cooperative agreement with an academic partner, the design, construction, and tenant build-out of a facility to support energy and minerals research and appurtenant associated structures.
The United States Geological Survey shall retain ownership of the facility and associated structures described in subsection (a).

subsection.heading: Agreements
subsection.chapeau: The Director may enter into agreements with, and to collect and expend funds or in-kind contributions from, academic, Federal, State, or other tenants over the life of the facility described in subsection (a) for the purposes of—

content.p: The Director may enter into a lease or other agreement with the academic partner with which the Director has entered into a cooperative agreement under subsection (a), at no cost to the Federal Government, to obtain land on which to construct the facility described in that subsection for a term of not less than 99 years.

subsection.heading: Reports
subsection.chapeau: The Director shall submit to Congress annual reports on—

paragraph.content: the facility described in subsection (a); and
the authorities used under this section.

content.p: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary of the Interior to carry out this section $167,000,000 for fiscal year 2022, to remain available until expended.

note.heading: Wage Rate Requirements
note.p: For provisions relating to rates of wages to be paid to laborers and mechanics on projects for construction, alteration, or repair work funded under div. D or an amendment by div. D of Pub. L. 117–58, including authority of Secretary of Labor, see section 18851 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare.
